issues_found: OMP experiment (C2) never escalated from pilot to full scale. C2 ran 3 letters x 30 tokens (90 total observations) at 97.8% baseline absorption rate — near-zero statistical power. The falsification criterion 'OMP >= 80% of FF rate' is trivially satisfied at 1.000 regardless of true effect. Bootstrap SE ≈ 0.037 per letter; a 2-3% true reduction is undetectable. The paper's 'decisively falsifies' and 'unambiguous' language is scientifically indefensible.

All core experiments remain in PILOT mode at 10-100x smaller scale than the methodology specifies. Files A1, A2, A3, B2, C2, D2 all carry mode='PILOT' in result JSON. C2 targeted 10,000 tokens/26 letters, got 90 tokens/3 letters. B1 targeted 200,000 tokens for co-occurrence, got 10,000 (5%). This is a systematic failure of the pilot-to-full escalation pipeline — the go/no-go gates passed but the full-scale triggers were never fired.

Cross-architecture 'replication' AUROC=0.837 uses proxy labels (decoder cosine >= 0.30 to letter probes, n_pos=77) that E1 and A3 confirm are NOT absorbed features — IG measurement shows 0% absorption in TopK-32k SAE. The paper presents this as cross-architecture replication in the abstract and Table 1, which is factually wrong. AUROC=0.757 at n_pos=18 gold IG labels is the only valid absorption detection claim.

EncNorm mechanistic narrative contradicted by own layer data. Paper claims gradient competition inflates ||w_enc,j||_2. A2 shows absorbed/non-absorbed ratio = 0.877 at L2, 1.055 at L4, 1.267 at L6, 0.891 at L8, 0.933 at L10. Signal inverts at 3 of 5 layers. Paper reports 'peaks at L6' as positive finding without addressing the inversions.

H3 cross-hierarchy experiment is invalid and absent. D2 shows entity-type absorption = 0.0% while negative control = 0.05%, direction opposite to H3. The D2 notes acknowledge 'Used entity-type probe on control sentences for comparison' — the negative control was measured with the wrong probe. The experiment cannot support any conclusion.

F1 width recovery (67%) is confounded by different hook points: Standard-24k uses resid_pre, TopK-32k uses resid_post. These are different activation spaces. Two absorbed features (2406, 11270) share best_matching_32k_idx=16435; two others (24154, 7371) share 29309 — possible false matches. The 67% figure is uninterpretable without a hook-confound baseline.

EncNorm independence from EDA not established. Spearman r(EncNorm, EDA) = 0.712, sharing 51% variance. AUROC improvement 0.757 vs 0.650 could reflect EncNorm's distributional shape being more favorable for severe class imbalance (18/24576) rather than independent information. No partial analysis was performed.

All results from GPT-2 Small (117M, 2019). Gemma Scope returned 401 Unauthorized. NeurIPS 2026 main track expects cross-model validation; interpretability literature centers on Gemma Scope, Llama-3. Single-model results with a 2019 model are a substantial weakness for main track submission.

O_jaccard AUROC=0.721 conflates features O_jaccard can detect (9 of 18, in letter set) with features where O_jaccard=0 by construction (other 9, not in letter set). Effective AUROC within the detectable subset is not reported. The B2 notes acknowledge this structural coverage limitation.

All 6 required figures (fig_teaser, fig_enc_norm_hist, fig_omp_design, fig_roc_curves, fig_ablation, fig_omp_results) are missing. LaTeX figures directory contains 5 PDFs from an older iteration (fig1_eda_method.pdf through fig5_phase_stability.pdf). The paper references non-existent figure files. This is a pre-submission blocker.
issues_fixed: Statistical toolkit correctly applied (bootstrap CIs, DeLong tests, Cohen's d, Mann-Whitney — all correctly implemented throughout iter_003 experiments)
Negative result reporting remains exemplary — D2 probe failure, D1 probe artifact, F1 hook-confound all reported honestly with specific numbers

Claim-evidence integrity improved: paper.md values cross-verified against source JSONs; all 12 spot-checked numerical claims are consistent with source data (DeLong z, AUROC CIs, Cohen's d, F1 recovery count)
Writing avoids banned patterns — no 'In recent years,' 'groundbreaking,' 'to the best of our knowledge'
Pre-committed falsification criterion is stated before reporting result — this methodological strength is maintained
Iter-002 lesson on EDA_norm vs EDA_base priority was partially addressed (EncNorm promoted as primary detector over raw EDA)
